comparisons.weak_hook: I learned something important about content this week.
comparisons.strong_hook: I almost deleted a post that became my highest-converting one. The reason matters if your expertise gets ignored online.
comparisons.annotation: Micro-cliffhanger + clear stakes. It creates curiosity without hiding the topic.

comparisons.weak_hook: Most founders should think differently about storytelling.
comparisons.strong_hook: A founder told me, 'We have better product proof than competitors, but buyers still pick them.' The problem wasn't product. It was the first 12 words.
comparisons.annotation: Specific scene, direct quote, and a curiosity gap anchored in a real business problem.

comparisons.weak_hook: Here are three tips for better LinkedIn writing.
comparisons.strong_hook: Three lines can quietly kill trust on LinkedIn. If your post opens with any of these, readers leave before your expertise appears.
comparisons.annotation: Promises concrete payoff and names a consequence, which increases read-through intent.

before_after.before: I want to share a lesson from a client engagement.
before_after.after: A client had world-class expertise and near-zero pipeline trust. One sentence in their intro was doing the damage.
before_after.annotation: Turns a flat announcement into a story opening with tension and implied resolution.

before_after.after: Generic messaging feels safe until a sophisticated buyer reads it and thinks, 'This could be anyone.'
before_after.annotation: Moves from abstract advice to a believable buyer moment.

before_after.before: I have an idea about writing better hooks.
before_after.after: Most hooks fail because they ask for attention before earning trust. Here's the structure that fixes that.
before_after.annotation: Creates a curiosity gap while clearly signaling payoff.
